summaryText: Guest Speaker Tommy 'Urban D' Kyllonen opened by celebrating the diversity of his family and church, then addressed the rapid changes and increasing division in our cities. He emphasized that as believers, we have the answer to this division and are called to love our neighbors as ourselves, a command that includes everyone regardless of background. Pastor Tommy set up the message with a spoken word piece highlighting the conditional love often practiced today, contrasting it with Jesus's teaching. He then turned to Luke 10, where an expert in religious law asks Jesus how to inherit eternal life and, seeking a loophole, asks 'Who is my neighbor?' In response, Jesus tells the parable of the Good Samaritan. Pastor Tommy explained the historical tension between Jews and Samaritans, noting that Jesus made the 'despised' Samaritan the hero of the story to show that our neighbor is anyone in need, especially those different from us. He shared stories from his church's 'Love Our City' initiative in Tampa, where serving diverse communities through practical acts of kindness opened doors for ministry and changed negative perceptions of the church. The message concluded with a call to action: to pray for neighbors by name, to love people different from ourselves by getting over our own fears, and to find creative opportunities to serve others, letting the Holy Spirit guide us to shine Christ's light in our communities.
mainPoints: Jesus defines 'neighbor' without loopholes, commanding us to love all people, especially those who are different from us.
Loving our neighbor is an expression of God's love for us and a practical way to change negative narratives about the church.
We are called to active, creative outreach through prayer, overcoming personal fear, and finding daily opportunities to serve.

versesMentioned.text: On one occasion an expert in the law stood up to test Jesus. 'Teacher,' he asked, 'what must I do to inherit eternal life?'
He answered, "'Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind'; and, 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'"
But he wanted to justify himself, so he asked Jesus, 'And who is my neighbor?'
In reply Jesus said: 'A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, when he was attacked by robbers. They stripped him of his clothes, beat him and went away, leaving him half dead.'
A priest happened to be going down the same road, and when he saw the man, he passed by on the other side. So too, a Levite, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
But a Samaritan, as he traveled, came where the man was; and when he saw him, he took pity on him.
'Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?' The expert in the law replied, 'The one who had mercy on him.' Jesus told him, 'Go and do likewise.'

Monday.action: Identify three people in your life who are different from you in some way (background, beliefs, etc.).
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to see my neighbors as you see them. Help me understand what it means to love them as I love myself.

Tuesday.scripture: Luke 10:29 - 'But he wanted to justify himself, so he asked Jesus, "And who is my neighbor?"'
Tuesday.action: Write down the name of one person you have previously excluded from your 'neighbor' list. Commit to praying for them this week.
Tuesday.prayer: God, reveal any loopholes I use to avoid loving certain people. Give me a heart that seeks to include, not exclude.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 10:33 - 'But a Samaritan... when he saw him, he took pity on him.'
Wednesday.action: Perform one small, anonymous act of kindness for a stranger or someone you find difficult.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me compassion that moves me to action. Let me see past differences to the human need in front of me.

Thursday.action: Look for a practical need you can meet for a neighbor, coworker, or family member today.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, you are my example. Give me the courage to 'go and do,' stepping out of my comfort zone to serve others.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 5:16 - 'Let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.'
Friday.action: Intentionally start a friendly conversation with someone new, with no agenda other than to show Christ's love.
Friday.prayer: Father, may my actions today point people to you. Use my words and deeds to shine your light in my city.

Saturday.scripture: Philippians 2:3-4 - 'Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility value others above yourselves, not looking to your own interests but each of you to the interests of the others.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on a recent time fear of rejection held you back. Plan one step to overcome that fear in the coming week.
Saturday.prayer: God, help me to get over myself. Humble my heart and make me more concerned with the interests of others than my own.
